ai_strategy.summary: UAlbany is one of the clearest AI-first R1 profiles in this sweep. AI Plus is an institution-wide strategy; SUNY invested $2.4M in the AI & Society College and AI & Society Research Center; UAlbany committed $37M to computing infrastructure including a $16.5M NVIDIA DGX supercomputer; and it is leading Empire AI SUNY partnerships.

key_provisions: Use AI in accordance with academic integrity expectations and instructor or unit guidance
Do not enter restricted, confidential, regulated, or sensitive data into unapproved AI tools
Review AI outputs for accuracy, hallucination, bias, copyright, and attribution issues
Disclose or cite AI use when required by course, research, publisher, or unit rules
Keep human judgment and accountability in consequential academic, research, and administrative decisions
ai_policy.summary: University at Albany's AI policy posture is best characterized as comprehensive: public sources show explicit expectations for responsible use, privacy, disclosure, academic integrity, and human review, while leaving some implementation decisions to units or instructors.

narrative_summary: University at Albany (Albany, NY) is an R1 institution whose public AI posture shows activity across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tooling, workforce readiness, and access. UAlbany is one of the clearest AI-first R1 profiles in this sweep. AI Plus is an institution-wide strategy; SUNY invested $2.4M in the AI & Society College and AI & Society Research Center; UAlbany committed $37M to computing infrastructure including a $16.5M NVIDIA DGX supercomputer; and it is leading Empire AI SUNY partnerships. The most concrete public initiatives captured here are AI Plus institution-wide strategy; AI & Society College; NVIDIA DGX AI supercomputer; AI & Society Research Center; Empire AI SUNY partnerships led by UAlbany. Across the profile, the strongest evidence comes from official university AI hubs, policy pages, teaching resources, research-center pages, and program pages rather than secondary reporting. The institution_type is set to r1_research from the canonical Carnegie R1 list. Known gaps are intentionally explicit: leadership names, user counts, detailed budgets, and compute counts are only included when they surfaced in public sources. Overall, the profile supports at least five AI initiatives and evidences strategy/leadership, governance, teaching/curriculum, research infrastructure, student services or enterprise tooling, workforce/community, partnerships, and equity dimensions where public sources permit.
